name: MIB-626 COVID-19 acute kidney injury randomized trial
summary: A 14-day randomized placebo-controlled trial of MIB-626 in hospitalized adults with COVID-19 and acute kidney injury, focused on NAD target engagement, kidney injury, inflammation, disease severity, and safety. The peer-reviewed result is represented locally, while ClinicalTrials.gov still had no registry results section in the June 14, 2026 API check.
population: Hospitalized adults with COVID-19 and acute kidney injury

trial_details.horizon_note: Results are already in the public evidence layer, so this is not an open no-result watch item.
trial_details.why_it_matters: Results matter for NAD and Redox Restoration because they can show whether the intervention changes NAD target engagement, kidney injury, inflammation, disease severity, and safety in hospitalized adults with COVID-19 and acute kidney injury. That is still narrower than proof of broad aging reversal.
